observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the Municipal Accountant:
In coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, to verify and validate the dormant accounts’ collectibility status; and
To facilitate the completion of the necessary documents and request for authority from the COA to write-off the dormant receivable accounts amounting to ₱1,668,116.80.

We commended the efforts of the Municipality of Basud for the compliance with the aforementioned Circular, the submission of complete and accurate reports and the successful reconciliation of Property records and accounting records.

We recommended that the LCE require the:
Municipal Engineer to trace and verify the status of the projects in relation to dormant CIP account balances, and submit copies of the documents such as Project Status Report and/or Certificate of Project Completion to the Municipal Accountant; and
Municipal Accountant, upon receipt of the submitted documents from the Municipal Engineer, make necessary adjustments in the books updating the status of the projects recorded under CIP accounts and reclassify the balance relative to all identified completed projects to appropriate asset accounts to achieve fair presentation of the financial statements.

We recommended that the LCE require the:
Municipal Assessor and GSO to undertake all the necessary procedures to secure the Certificate of Title of its land properties to validate its ownership and to protect the interest of the Municipality from adverse claims by third parties; and
Municipal Accountant to properly disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the titling status of its land properties.

We recommended that the LCE require the GSO to label all LGU properties with inventory tags/stickers on the prominent but secure portion of the property for a more facilitative tracking and identification of PPE items.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all amounts withheld from the salaries of LGU officials and employees due to GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth be remitted in full within the prescribed period to prevent under remittances.

We recommended the LCE require the Municipal Engineer and MPDC to:
Implement all pending development projects targeted under the 20 per cent LDF for CY 2024; and
Ensure that all development projects to be funded out of the 20 per cent LDF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready, by continuously pursuing improved practices in planning, programming and implementation of programs and projects.
We recommended that the LCE require the Local DRRM Officer to monitor the implementation of PPAs from continuing LDRRMF, and the STF to facilitate prompt completion.

We recommended that the LCE require the GAD Focal Person to ensure timely submission of the GPB and GAD AR to Audit Team in accordance with the provision of Section V of COA Circular No. 2014-001, to timely monitor and evaluate the utilization of GAD Fund.

We recommended that the LCE require all Implementing Office to:
Ensure that their PPAs are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready; and
For any unexpected changes, revise the Project Procurement Management Plans and submit to the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat for consolidation and submission to Government Procurement Policy Board pursuant to RA No. 9184.

We recommended that the LCE require the BAC and the Municipal Engineer to establish a robust monitoring system that ensures the contractor's compliance with the requirements stated in Section 62 of 2016 R-IRR of RA No. 9184 to ensure that the procuring entity can recover its expenses for any restoration or reconstruction works necessitated by structural defects or failure that may arise during the applicable warranty period.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

Prepare the Schedule of Dormant Accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2023-008; 

Coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to verify and validate the Dormant accounts’ collectibility status; and

Facilitate the completion of the necessary documents to request the write-off of dormant accounts from the COA.
observations.managementAction: Management committed to comply with the recommendation.
observations.reason: The Management disclosed that on June 2024, they have already written off dormant accounts that are below ₱100,000.00.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

In coordination with the Municipal Engineering Office, direct the Property Units to conduct a complete physical count and assessment of all public infrastructure assets of the LGU; and

Require the Accounting and Property Units to ensure that all PPEs included in the RPCPPE are duly recorded in their respective records, and ensure that Property and Ledger Cards are reconciled accordingly.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Disposal Committee to institute measures for the immediate and proper disposal of all obsolete and unserviceable properties in accordance with pertinent rules and regulations. Also, once disposal has been undertaken, the Municipal Accountant should derecognize in the books said unserviceable properties.

We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant provide the corresponding depreciation expenses of all PPEs with no depreciation, using the determined estimated useful lives provided in the Local Government Accounting Manual and straight-line method of depreciation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

Advise the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC)/BAC-Technical Working Group to thoroughly verify, validate, and ascertain that all the statements and documents submitted by the bidder with the Lowest Calculated Bid including the entries made to determine and ensure the technical and operational competence of the winning contractors during the post-qualification evaluation; and

Direct the BAC to refrain from exercising discretion in the conduct of post-qualification to avoid possible disallowance in the audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

In coordination with the BAC and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC), identify and address the difficulties encountered in project implementation to avoid incurrence in the future;

Instruct the MPDC to closely monitor and track the implementation of the projects of the Municipality in accordance with the approved development plan, promptly informing the former, BAC and implementing office of any deviations; and

Ensure that the Municipal Engineer implements the projects funded under LGSF-SBDP of the NTF-ELCAC within the prescribed timeline.

We recommended that the BAC:

Require the concerned contractors to post performance securities prior to the signing of the contract; and

Disqualify the contractor, or bidder with the Lowest Calculated Responsive Bid or Highest Rated Responsive Bid in case of failure, refusal, or inability to post the required performance security.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

Require the BAC in coordination with the Implementing Units to thoroughly establish the actual needs of the latter;

Require the BAC to review the technical requirements/descriptions of the project prior to posting; and

Direct the BAC to refrain from exercising discretion in the conduct of preliminary examination of bids and post-qualification to avoid possible disallowance in the audit.

We recommended that the BAC strictly adhere to the provision of R-IRR of RA No. 9184 on sending invitations to observers in all stages of the procurement to enhance transparency in operations and attach copies thereof in claims relative to projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and BAC adhere to the provisions prescribed in the 2016 R-IRR of RA No. 9184, requiring a warranty security on the procurement of goods. Henceforth, ensure that the procurement of goods During payments for procured goods be deducted with retention money or require a special bank guarantee to answer for any discovered manufacturing defect.
observations.managementAction: The BAC regularly discuss the warranty requirement in pre-bid conferences and BAC secretariat regularly reminds the suppliers on the matter

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant maintain SL for each control account in the General Ledger.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC to require contractors of infrastructure projects, for contracts signed after the state of calamity was lifted during CY 2023, to submit the required Performance Security format as specified in R-IRR of RA No. 9184. Additionally, furnish a copy of this document to the Audit Team for records and compliance review.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor moving forward, require the Human Resource Management Office to furnish the COA with copies of Contracts and its supporting documents within five working days after execution thereof in accordance with COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

Direct the BAC to thoroughly check the details, particularly issuance and validity dates, of irrevocable standby letters of credit of from a commercial bank, bank guarantees or surety bonds furnished by the contractors; and

Direct the BAC to ensure that the purpose of the irrevocable standby letters of credit, bank guarantees and/or surety bonds, to be posted in favor of the Government indicated the purpose for which the retention is intended, i.e., to cover uncorrected discovered defects and third-party liabilities.

We recommended that the Head of the Procuring Entity advise the BAC/BAC-Technical Working Group to thoroughly verify, validate and ascertain all the statements and documents submitted by the bidder including the entries made to determine and ensure the technical competence of the winning contractors during the post-qualification evaluation.

We recommend the Management to:

Require the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of the Local Government Unit’s (LGU’s) PPE annually;
Prepare and submit in a prescribed format a complete and accurate reports of Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) for the current year to the Municipal Accountant and the COA before January 31 of the succeeding year; and
Reconcile the physical count of the PPE with book records and make necessary adjustments.
observations.managementAction:
Management committed coordinated physical inventory for 2022.

Management committed preparation of RPCPPE after the physical count in 2022.




Reconciliation will follow after physical count in 2022.
observations.reason:
Management were not able to conduct physical count of PPEs for 2021.

RPCPPE were not prepared because of the lack of physical count.




Reconciliation were not done due to lack of physical count.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Engineer to implement a sound and effective monitoring system in place to ensure contractor’s compliance of the requirements of Section 62 of the 2016 R-IRR of RA No. 9184, in order to ensure that the procuring entity will reimburse its expenses in the restoration or reconstruction works in case of Structural Defects/Failure occurring during the applicable warranty period.
observations.managementAction: The Management agreed and promised to comply with the recommendation.
observations.reason: Municipal Engineer is still to establish Team for a sound and effective monitoring to ensure that the contractors comply with Section 62 of the R-IRR of R.A. 9184, and be held liable in case that structural defects/failure occur during the warranty period.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Maximize the utilization of 20 per cent LDF in attaining desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes for the benefit of the community; and
Ensure that all development projects to be funded out of the 20 per cent LDF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready by continue pursuing improved practices in planning, programming and implementing programs and projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer reconcile their Cash Local Treasury and Advances for Payroll account balances to ensure the reliability of the balances reflected in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
Ensure preparation and submission of these RAAFs in accordance with the cited regulation by requiring each AOs to prepare and submit RAAF for their accountability which is the basis of the consolidated RAAF; and
Include the receipt and issuance of checks in the RAAF.

We recommended that Municipal Accountant monitor the status of transferred funds to identify unutilized balances of completed PPAs and comply with COA Circular No. 94-013 on the return of the unexpended balances.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor remind the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to thoroughly verify, validate and ascertain the bidder’s NFCC to ensure that the bidder can sustain the operating cash flow of the transaction and to avoid possible award of contract to incapable bidders.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer submit STE as of the date of target completion for the project and all suspension orders or any documents that would warrant the non-imposition of liquidated damages to the concerned contractor to the Office of the Municipal Accountant copy furnished to the Audit Team. Otherwise, direct the Municipal Accountant to compute the liquidated damages based on the provisions cited in the foregoing project.

We recommended the Management to:

Ensure that the functions, responsibilities and accountabilities of the Officer-in-Charge-Municipal Treasurer (OIC-MT) are realistic and within her limits;

Strictly observe the Receipts and Collection Process embodied in Section 35, Volume I of MNGAS for LGUs; and

Reconcile the Report of Collection and Deposits (RCDs) during the OIC-MT’s term with the unrecorded deposits, if possible, else, make the OIC-MT accountable for the net under-remittance, if any, and make the necessary adjusting entries in the books to properly present the Cash Accounts of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Management committed on the implementation of the recommendation.
observations.reason: Discussed in the body of this Annual Audit Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant to refrain from recognizing and recording cost incurred for the projects under construction or not yet completed directly to PPE line item accounts other than Construction in Progress.

We recommended the Management to:

Instruct the Municipal Engineer to prepare an Inspection Report and/or Certificate of Completion for all completed projects at end of every year regardless if no contractor’s billings were received yet. Subsequently, forward the copies of the same to the Municipal Accountant as basis of recording; and

Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare necessary journal entries, upon receipt of documents from the Municipal Engineer, to properly recognize completed projects in the books as asset and the corresponding obligation as liability at the end of every year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer
exert extra effort to trace the reconciling items and make necessary adjustments in the
books of accounts to reflect the correct balance of the Cash in Bank and other affected
accounts for fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor and
the Municipal Treasurer to exert extra effort to facilitate the transfer of title of land in the
name of the Municipal Government of Basud, Camarines Norte to protect the interest of
the government and to document its ownership.

We reiterated the prior year’s audit recommendations that the Municipal Mayor
require the Inventory Team/Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit a complete and
accurate report.

We also recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and the
Municipal Treasurer fo maintain complete records, property ledger cards and stocks
cards, respectively, and intensify review and reconciliation of those records to come up
with reconciled balances of PPE accounts.

We recommended that:

a. The Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal
Accountant to immediately remit all overdue taxes withheld totalling
P169,997.13 as of December 31, 2016 pursuant to RA. No. 7649 dated April
6, 1993 and RMO No. 8-2003 dated April 1, 2003 of the BIR and ensure the
timely remittance of all subsequent taxes withheld; and

b. Any penalties/surcharges t the BIR may impose for the late remittances shall
be a personal liability of the officials or employees who caused such delay.

We recommended that Management strictly enforce the existing regulation
pertaining to salary deductions and adopt control measures by not permitting any
additional Ioan payments to be deducted on the salaries of those who are receiving
minimal net take home pay, to ensure compliance with the General Provisions of the
GAA for FY 2016.

We recommended that management:

a. Require the Department Heads to distinguish their GAD related activities
especially the Municipal Health Office, Municipal Social Welfare Office,
with the help of the Municipal Budget Officer, to facilitate the formdation of
GPB with projects and activities equal to at least five percent of the total
appropriation of the municipality;

b.  Follow the Calendar Schedule for the preparation of GPB for LGUs and
ensure that a DILG-approved GPB is incorporated in the Annual Budget.
Also, make sure that the former will not hinder the timely preparation of the
latter; and

c. Implement applicable provisions in the convention on the elimination of all
forms of discrimination against women and promote gender equality within
the municipality.

We recommended that the management optimize the utilization of the Mitigation
Fund of the municipality in order not to deprive the constituents of the potential benefits
out of disaster preparedness. Moreover, the management should appropriately state the
programs/projects/activities and give priority to the procurement of basic rescue and
response equipment and conduct of municipal-wide trainings.

We recommended that the:

a.  Management utilize immediately the capitation fund subsidized by the
Philhealth Insurance Corporation and use the said fund for its specific
purpose stipulated in the Philheatlh Circulars, in compliance with Section
309(b) of RA No. 7160; and

b.  Accounting Office maintain SL per package/program to properly monitor the
receipts, disbursements and optimum wtilization of funds for early benefits of
the constituents.

We recommended the Management to coordinate with the depository bank to verify the time deposit account and to secure the Bank Certificate or Passbook. Also, the Municipal Treasurer should maintain updated Bank Certificate or Passbook which should be reconciled periodically with the account kept by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 According to the concerned personnel to handle this very meticulous work and regarding lack of stock room to store the undistributed supplies, they cannot address this situation.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous 2015 recommendations that: Management require the GSO- Designate and the Inventory committee to complete the physical inventory of PPE and to prepare and submit complete and updated report thereon to determine the accuracy and correctness of the year-end balance; Management require Inventory committee to conduct a thorough review based on the source documents of the transactions from the assumption of the Municipal Accountant in year 2002 and the reason of discrepancies is due to non-turnover of records by the former Accountant. As to Road Networks and other Infrastructure programs and projects as contrary to EO. No. 431 dated May 30, 2005 and Department of Finance (DOF), Department of Budget and Management (DBM) and COA Joint Circular No. 4-2012 dated September 11, 2012, in order not to burden the liability account of the Municipality.

No. 431 dated May 30, 2005 and DOF, DBM and COA Joint Circular No. 4-2012 dated September 11, 2012 in order not to burden the liability account of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: Management require submission of the Monthly Report of Official Travels and the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption to the Office of the Auditor; ensure that Driver’s Trip Tickets (DTTs) submitted are duly accomplished and attached to disbursement vouchers; all fuel gasoline for each type of vehicle with only authorized driver to withdraw gasoline; all heavy equipment supported with program of work and bill of materials and utilization in hours used should be indicated in DTT; as a general rule, purchases should be made through competitive public bidding; alternative mode of procurement may be resorted to if conditions were met in accordance to Section 50, Rule XVI of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (R-IRR) of R.A. observations.recommendation: We recommended the management to optimize the utilization of the Mitigation Fund of the municipality in order not to deprive the constituents of the potential benefits out of disaster preparedness. Moreover, the management should give priority to the procurement of basic rescue and response equipment and conduct of municipal-wide trainings.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 According to the Budget Officer, her office called the attention of Disaster Risk Management (DRRM) Management. The DRRM Officer Designate justified that expenditures were used for end-to-end monitoring, forecasting, early warning system, research and education campaign program and up-to-date reporting for DRRM. The LGU received approval of SP Resolution No. 213-2015, specifically Condition No.5. The DRRM Officer is finalizing the five year comprehensive DRRM Plan which includes the utilization of the unutilized DRRM Funds from 2012 to 2016. The Plan and the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) for the unobligated funds has been already reprogrammed for presentation to LDRRM Council for subsequent approval of the Sangguniang Bayan so that the funds could be fully utilized.
observations.reason: Delayed creation and staffing of MDRRMO; lack of knowledgeable personnel and protracted processes for approval.

observations.reason: Knowingly most protective services included in the Financial Plan are responsive to promotion of women's participation but not put into formal plan; lack of submissions from other offices.
